blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I want to come to the appointments.
He turns to look at her. His eyes are red at the edges — she realizes he has been fighting that since the river, holding it back while he sat with everything she'd given him.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: The monitoring ones. The check-ins. I want to be there.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: You said you've always gone alone. You don't have to go alone anymore.

The simplicity of it. The specific, practical, un-performed simplicity of it — not a declaration about forever, not a promise to fix anything — just: I will be there. In the beige-walled room. I will sit in the chair next to you.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You don't know what you're signing up for.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: You just told me what I'm signing up for. I was listening.
The gondola rocks. The wheel begins to descend. The carnival rises toward them.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I'm not trying to save you, Cass. I know I can't. I just don't want you to be alone in those rooms anymore.
